resources.title: AERO Ochre Science Year 8 Unit 1 - Cells, tissues and organs
resources.summary: A comprehensive 15-lesson sequence covering cells, tissues, and organs, with specific lessons (4-8) dedicated to animal and plant cell structure and function.
follow_up_questions: How can we relate the structure of plant cell walls to the physical properties of plants?
In what ways do specialised cells in plants and animals differ in their function?

resources.title: 5 native plants that illuminate deep Aboriginal knowledge
resources.summary: An article exploring the features and uses of native Australian plants, highlighting deep Aboriginal knowledge and its importance in understanding ecosystems.
follow_up_questions: How does First Nations knowledge of plants complement Western biological classification?
What specific plant structures might be important for the medicinal uses described?

resources.title: Comparing animal and plant cells (video) - Khan Academy
resources.summary: A clear, concise video explaining the fundamental similarities and differences between plant and animal cells, focusing on organelles.
follow_up_questions: What are the primary differences between plant and animal cells?
Why do plant cells require a cell wall?

resources.title: Lesson: Comparing Cells
resources.summary: An interactive lesson comparing four types of cells (animal, plant, bacteria, yeast) with 3D models and diagrams.
follow_up_questions: How do 3D models help us understand the function of organelles?
What are the differences between animal, plant, bacteria, and yeast cells?

resources.title: Year 8 Science – Cells & Microscopes (Flashcards Australia) - Tes
resources.summary: Printable science flashcards aligned to the Australian Curriculum (ACSSU149) for retrieval practice on cell structures and microscopes.
follow_up_questions: What are the key organelles to remember for Year 8?
How do microscopes help us see these structures?

resources.title: What are plant and animal cells? - BBC Bitesize
resources.summary: A structured guide with interactive activities and quizzes on the structure and function of plant and animal cells.
follow_up_questions: What gives plant cells their crunch?
How do animal and plant cells differ in their structure?

resources.title: Science Teacher Background Information - Seasonal Knowledge
resources.summary: Teacher background information on how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples' seasonal knowledge relates to the management of plant and animal resources.
follow_up_questions: How does seasonal knowledge inform the management of plant and animal resources?
Why is it important to integrate First Nations knowledge into science curricula?

resources.title: 6.1 What is the particle model of matter? - Siyavula
resources.summary: A clear explanation of the particle model of matter, covering solids, liquids, and gases, relevant to ACSSU152.
follow_up_questions: How does the particle model explain the states of matter?
How can we use the particle model to understand diffusion in cells?

resources.title: Introduction to the Particle Theory of Matter - Let's Talk Science
resources.summary: An introduction to the particle theory of matter, explaining how it serves as a scientific model for understanding matter.
follow_up_questions: What are the key tenets of the particle theory of matter?
How do particles behave in different states?

resources.title: Plant science: Indigenous Knowledge - UQ Library Guides
resources.summary: A library guide providing resources and context on Indigenous knowledge systems related to plant science and land management.
follow_up_questions: How does Indigenous knowledge of flora and fauna differ from Western scientific classification?
How can we incorporate these perspectives into our science lessons?

curriculum_outcomes.description: Cells are the basic units of living things; they have specialised structures and functions
Differences between elements, compounds and mixtures can be described at a particle level

search_queries_used: Year 8 Science plant and animal cells ACSSU149 Australian curriculum
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander perspectives on biology and cells for schools
Indigenous knowledge systems and biological classification for students
ABC Education Year 8 Science cells plant animal differences
Science by Doing Year 8 Cells and Systems
Australian Academy of Science Year 8 Science cells resources
First Nations perspectives on plants and animals for science curriculum
differences between plant and animal cells interactive activity Year 8
particle model of matter ACSSU152 Year 8 Science resources
First Nations knowledge of plant biology and ecosystems for science education
ai_recommendation: The most valuable resource is the AERO Ochre Science Year 8 Unit 1, which provides a comprehensive, curriculum-aligned sequence of lessons on cells that builds understanding from first principles. I recommend pairing this with the '5 native plants that illuminate deep Aboriginal knowledge' article from Cosmos Magazine to integrate authentic First Nations perspectives on plant biology and ecological relationships into your classroom discussions.
